data.title: Novo is slowly catching up with Lilly: Is Wegovy pill fueling the rally
data.description:
Despite reaching multiyear lows earlier this year due to a disappointing outlook, Novo Nordisk (NVO) is closing the gap with its main rival, Eli Lilly (LLY), with shares of the Danish drugmaker adding 22% over the past three months compared to the U.S. pharma giant’s ~5% rise.

Many analysts attribute the upsurge to the Wegovy pill, an oral version of the company’s blockbuster weight loss injectable semaglutide launched earlier this year. While Eli Lilly (LLY) introduced its competing product, Foundayo, last month, the Zepbound maker seems to be struggling to negate Novo’s (NVO) first-mover advantage.

U.S. prescriptions for the two once-daily pills, a closely watched metric indicating their early commercial success, appear to favor Novo (NVO), according to IQVIA (IQV) data sourced by analysts.

During the first week of their respective launches, the Wegovy pill racked up roughly 18,410 U.S. prescriptions in January, while Foundayo garnered only 3,707 scripts last month.

“Despite Wall Street skepticism, Novo Nordisk A/S is starting to catch up with Eli Lilly, the market leader in the GLP-1s market,” claimed SA analyst ALLKA Research, reiterating a Strong Buy rating on the stock.

The analyst argued that since the FDA approved the Wegovy pill in December, Novo (NVO) “has been gradually recovering lost ground in the GLP-1 market, while demand for its rival, Lilly's (LLY) Foundayo (orforglipron), is still weak since its launch in April.”

Analyst Manika Premsingh, who has a Buy rating on NVO, agreed, noting that the Wegovy pill, as well as Novo's (NVO) newly launched Ozempic pill for diabetes, boosted its shares last month. “While the company is still not out of the woods, the first fruits of its labor to get back on track are beginning to show,” Premsingh opined.

However, Eli Lilly (LLY), which has added approximately 30% this month compared to Novo’s (NVO) 13% gain, received a slew of bullish views on Seeking Alpha recently, including from Premsingh, who upgraded the stock to Strong Buy from Buy this week.

The analyst acknowledged the potential for LLY to slide on the back of its recent M&A spree and Foundayo’s slow ramp-up. “But the bigger picture is overwhelmingly positive right now,” Premsingh wrote, forecasting a 30%–50% upside for the stock thanks to its latest financials and upgraded outlook.

data.title: SA Asks: What are the best prediction market stocks right now?
data.description:
What are the best prediction market stocks right now for investors?

Seeking Alpha analysts The J Thesis and Caffital Research offer their picks.

The J Thesis: Quite honestly, I’d have to go with the biggest names, such as Robinhood (HOOD), Interactive Brokers (IBKR), and Coinbase (COIN). Why? They have the biggest platforms, alongside the largest user bases. I think that’s important and could accelerate prediction market adoption. These platforms also have solid reputations, and that’s significant for new products.

Prediction markets are becoming an important tool to forecast the future. I think the segment remains in the early innings, and that’s why adoption remains crucial. I think prediction markets will eventually become an offering to an everything app. That’s why I’d go with the best platforms out there.

Caffital Research: I believe that DraftKings (DKNG) and Flutter Entertainment (FLUT) are the best prediction market stocks. These picks do seem unintuitive on the surface; the current market narrative is that as the two U.S. sports betting leaders, the companies are disadvantaged by competition arising from prediction market platforms.

What makes DraftKings and Flutter attractive picks are the companies’ own entries into prediction markets. DraftKings Predictions and FanDuel Predicts increase the companies’ market reach into new states and contract categories while benefiting from the brands’ previous billion-dollar marketing investments that have created widespread brand recognition. 

For now, disruption from other prediction market platforms has been minimal. Both stocks have been on a sharp downward trend, pricing in the rise of prediction markets as a significant headwind but forgetting the market launches as a potential tailwind.

  • Top Financial Exchanges and Data Stocks

data.title: Real estate stocks lose out in May, health care REITs notable laggards
data.description:
Real estate stocks lost out to the broader markets in May, ending in the red on average, while the S&P 500 index added over 5%.

Wall Street gained from signs of easing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, stronger-than-expected jobs report, strong corporate earnings, and renewed optimism surrounding the AI-linked stocks.

However, persistent inflation worries continued to drive the Treasury yields higher. The benchmark U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) closed the month 5 basis points higher at 4.44%.

Kevin Warsh was sworn in as the 17th chairman of the Federal Reserve this month. Treasury yields fluctuated as investors considered whether the Fed may need to keep policy tighter amid lingering inflation concerns.

National debt is now larger than its economic output. As of March 31, the ratio of publicly held debt to GDP was 100.2%, compared with 99.5% when the last fiscal year ended on September 30, 2025.

For the impact on the stock market, investors don't appear to be concerned for now. "The reason the market keeps rising can be summed up in a few words: earnings growth," said Deep Value Investing.

Meanwhile, real estate stocks have been impacted by fiscal concerns that weighed on U.S. bonds and increased borrowing costs.

The S&P 500 Real Estate Index Sector (SP500-60) declined 1.17% month-over-month in May to 279.06 points, while the accompanying State Street Real Estate Select Sector SPDR ETF (XLRE) decreased 0.74% to $43.99.

The Dow Jones REIT Indx Equity REIT Total Return Index (REIT:IND) added 0.04%, while the FTSE Nareit All Equity REITs index retreated 0.08%.

Health care REITs stood out as notable laggards during the month of May.

Weekly Winners & Losers

Among largecap losers, CoStar Group (CSGP) led the pack, retreating 5.10% to $32.20.

The week saw the real estate services provider enter into a definitive agreement to acquire Zonda for a cash consideration of $800M. Zonda provides new home construction data, homebuilder software, and residential real estate marketplaces, expanding CSGP's reach into a major new segment of the real estate industry.

Welltower (WELL) (-4.94% W/W to $205.33) and CBRE Group (CBRE) (-4.58% W/W to $125.04) were the next on the list.

Welltower CFO Timothy McHugh was appointed to the board of FrontView REIT (FVR) this week.

Lineage (LINE) led the gainers in the category, advancing 10.89% week-over-week to $44.41.

Among midcap stocks, Diversified Healthcare Trust (DHC) (-6.52% W/W to $8.32) and National Health Investors (NHI) (-4.26% W/W to $73.29) led the losers.

National Health Investors held its annual meeting of stockholders on Wednesday, electing all seven director nominees. The week also saw Seeking Alpha analyst Jussi Askola choose the healthcare REIT as a bargain amid a significant transformation at the company. The company is divesting skilled nursing facilities to focus on the undersupplied senior housing market.

Fermi (FRMI) (+15.18% W/W to $6.98) topped the gainers.

Opendoor Technologies (OPEN) was the second-biggest winner, adding 10.28% from the prior week to $5.04. OPEN benefited from the news that it is set to be added to the Russell 3000 index.

Chiron Real Estate (XRN) led the smallcap losers, declining 3.77% week-over-week to $36.04. Recently, Chiron Real Estate cut its dividend by 36% to $0.16, reallocating capital toward high-return portfolio expansion.

LTC Properties (LTC) (-3.18% W/W to $37.41) and Community Healthcare Trust (CHCT) (-2.82% W/W to $17.22) followed in the list.

Claros Mortgage Trust (CMTG) (+11.47% W/W to $2.43) led the gainers in the category.

Here is a look at the subsector performances for the week and the month:

data.title: SA Asks: What's next for BP after its chairman's ouster?
data.description:
BP (BP) shares took a hit this past week after the energy company announced its board had ousted its chairman, Albert Manifold, over governance and conduct issues, allegations that he has strongly denied. 

Manifold's departure comes roughly eight months after he was appointed chairman and less than two months after Meg O’Neill was installed as BP's new CEO, its fifth since 2020. His ouster also comes a little over a year after the company announced plans to shift its focus back to fossil fuels and away from renewable energy.

We asked Seeking Alpha analysts IWA Research, Gary Gambino, and Manika Premsingh what they thought lay ahead for the British oil and gas company. 

IWA Research: Although I'll note that this is actually the second high-profile dismissal in just three years—the previous one being the CEO at the time—I believe this marks an ungraceful end to a weak era for BP. Now BP (BP) can fully focus on restoring market confidence as they shift back to fossil fuels under its new CEO, Meg O’Neill, which has done wonders at Woodside Energy (WDS). The market could be pleasantly surprised by the moves of what I believe to be one of the most capable CEOs in oil today, supporting a potential re-rating.

Gary Gambino: The departure of a non-executive chairman doesn’t change much for BP (BP). The company has already reversed its diversification into lower-returning renewable energy projects and will continue to simplify the organization under new CEO Meg O’Neill, lowering complexity and costs. BP has projects underway to get back to a target of 100% reserves replacement by 2027. Exploration successes offshore in Brazil and the Gulf of America provide further opportunities to maintain production for the long term. 

Manika Premsingh: BP's (BP) firing of Chairman Albert Manifold for questionable behavior is concerning, given its recent history of instability at the management level. Current CEO Meg O'Neill's predecessor also departed abruptly for unknown reasons, and the one before that stepped down for failing to disclose personal relationships in the workplace. These continued developments reflect distracting cultural stresses for the company, which can be a particular drag at a time when it's already expected to see challenges. 

To be fair, stockwise, BP has done better than its British peer Shell (SHEL) year-to-date, with 20% gains compared with SHEL's 14%. However, it's still behind the 27% increase seen for the energy sector, as reflected in the performance of the State Street Energy Select Sector SPDR ETF (XLE).

The gap between BP and XLE's performance could well increase going forward due to the latest development, unless the company gains undeniable fundamental support from production or prices that reflect in its financial performance or outlook or both. 

Manika Premsingh is the leader of the SA investing group Green Growth Giants. She also runs the SA profile Long Term Tips. 

  • Top Integrated Oil and Gas Stocks

data.title: Insider trades: Baidu, Target among notable names
data.description:
This week, transactions were seen across firms including Baidu (BIDU), Target (TGT), and Enphase Energy (ENPH). The following trades occurred between May 25 and May 29.

  • Target (TGT) Executive Officer Brian Cornell sold 50,000 shares in the range of $129.84 to $130.54 each for a total of $6.49M. The sale represented roughly 13.3% of his holdings, reducing his stake to 327,271 shares.
  • Baidu (BIDU) director Jixun Foo disposed of 100% of his direct holdings in the company. He sold 122,584 shares at a transaction cost of $16.32 each, with the sale valued at $2M.
  • Delta Air Lines (DAL) EVP and President International, Alain Bellemare, sold 25,000 shares at $81.44 apiece for ~$2.04M. The divestment represented nearly 20.8% of his direct holdings, reducing his position to 95,025 shares. Meanwhile, the firm’s Chief People Officer, Allison Ausband, sold 10,000 shares in the range of $79 to $80.9 each for $799,500. The transaction accounted for about 7.2% of her direct holdings, leaving her with 128,854 shares.
  • Applied Materials (AMAT) director Judy Bruner sold 1,128 shares at $450 each, with the transaction valued at $507,600. The sale represented roughly 4% of her holdings, reducing them to 27,285 shares.
  • Unity Software (U) CEO and President Matthew Bromberg sold 138,993 shares at $27.18 each for ~$3.78M. The transaction accounted for nearly 8.2% of his direct holdings, leaving him with 1.56M shares.
  • MercadoLibre (MELI) director Alejandro Aguzin purchased 600 shares at $1,655 to $1,656.1 each for a total of $993,556. Following the transaction, his holdings increased to 5,355 shares.
  • Enphase Energy (ENPH) President and CEO Badrinarayanan Kothandaraman acquired 5,000 shares at a transaction cost of $67.50 each, with the purchase valued at $337,482. The purchase lifted his holdings to 1.75M shares.
  • Prospect Capital (PSEC) Chief Executive Officer John Barry bought a total of 865,000 shares in three different transactions for a total value of ~$1.99M. The purchase increased his total holdings to 87.55M shares.
  • Charles Schwab (SCHW) President and CEO Richard Wurster acquired 21,959 shares at $84.23 each, with the transaction valued at $1.85M. Following the purchase, his holdings stood at 223,004 shares.

data.title: Microsoft to unveil first Windows PCs powered by Nvidia chips next week: Axios
data.description:
Microsoft (MSFT) will join Nvidia (NVDA) at two key industry conferences next week to launch the first Windows computers that use Nvidia’s (NVDA) chips as the main processor, Axios reported on Saturday, citing people familiar with the matter.

At the events, the Computex trade show in Taiwan and Microsoft's Build developer conference in San Francisco, the companies will debut their joint work and the first Microsoft (MSFT) computers running on Nvidia (NVDA) chips.

Nvidia (NVDA) teased the launch in an X post on Friday, noting "A new era of PC" and citing coordinates to a location in Taipei, Taiwan, where the Computex trade show is scheduled to take place from June 1 to June 5.

Meanwhile, Microsoft's (MSFT) head for Windows and Devices, Pavan Davuluri, created excitement around the launch. “Something new is coming for developers,” he said in an X post on Friday, adding, “And no, it’s not a new OS version. See you at Build next week!”

Microsoft (MSFT) couldn’t be reached for comments outside business hours. Nvidia (NVDA) didn’t immediately respond to requests for comments.

data.title: America's Car-Mart retains advisor to evaluate strategic alternatives
data.description:
• America's Car-Mart (CRMT) said its board retained Houlihan Lokey Capital to serve as its financial advisor as the company continues to evaluate and pursue strategic alternatives, including potential financing, recapitalization, mergers and acquisitions, and other transactions.
  • The board also established a special committee to oversee strategic alternatives and appointed Adam Paul as an independent director and chair of the special committee, the company said in an  8-K filing on Friday.
  • The company also said on May 22 it increased the size of its board to 10 members from nine and appointed Paul as an independent director.
  • Shares of America's Car Mart (CRMT) have plunged 75% over the past year.

data.title: Notable analyst calls this week: Sandisk, Snowflake and Sensata among top picks
data.description:
Wall Street’s major market indexes ended higher on Friday, after the week saw more updates related to the U.S.-Iran conflict, as well as earnings reports from names including Dell and Salesforce.

Wall Street had a slew of upgrades and downgrades from analysts. Here are some of the major calls for the week:

Sandisk gets bullish rating from Barclays

Barclays upgraded Sandisk (SNDK) to Overweight and raised its price target to $2,300 per share.

“We see Memory/Storage as the most attractive vertical below accelerators,” Barclays analyst Tom O'Malley wrote. “We note continued upside to pricing with supply/demand imbalance persisting through CY27 & discuss below how the changes in contracts transform the nature of the next several years in the industry.”

O'Malley said Sandisk has been the “most aggressive and structurally innovative” among its peers in how it has handled contracts.

Susquehanna also upped its price targets on Sandisk to $3,250 from $2,000 and Micron (MU) to $1,750 from $600, citing continued strength in memory and storage.

Snowflake upgraded after massive earnings-led rally

Snowflake (SNOW) was upgraded at HSBC, one day after the stock jumped more than 36% due to stronger-than-expected results and guidance and a deal with Amazon.

“We upgrade our rating to Buy (from Hold) on stronger earnings momentum, improved AI monetization visibility, and greater confidence that CoCo can improve Snowflake’s long-term growth trajectory, while maintain strong margin discipline,” HSBC analyst Stephen Bersey said.

Bersey set a target price of $289, up from $176 for the stock.

BofA boosts price target for Apple

Apple (AAPL) was in the spotlight as Bank of America upped its price target to $380 from $330 on the tech giant, citing a potential boost from agentic artificial intelligence.

“If AI assistants become the new front door to search, apps, commerce, scheduling, payments, and workflow completion, we think Apple should have meaningful leverage over model providers, app developers, merchants, advertisers, and payment networks,” said BofA analyst Wamsi Mohan.

Delving deeper, Mohan said that the company's benefit in agentic AI comes from its semiconductors and iOS, as Apple's silicon can dictate what happens on a devices and iOS will determine how the AI is used by the user. 

Similarly, Evercore upped its price target on storage company Western Digital (WDC) to $575 from $550 and said it is “underappreciated” by investors.

“We continue to think WD (and the HDD industry) is a critical and still underappreciated component of the ongoing AI infrastructure buildout,” analyst Amit Daryanani wrote and maintained his Outperform rating.

“We remain constructive on WDC following investor meetings and believe their top line growth can outpace EB growth with EPS growth [greater than] revenue growth over the medium term driven by margin expansion and cap allocation,” he added.

Sensata Technologies (ST) was downgraded to Equal Weight from Overweight by Barclays due in part to its recent rally. "Much of the appreciation in the share price, we believe, has been due to enthusiasm regarding emerging data center exposure at ST, but this is not likely to contribute meaningfully to revenue until mid-2027," said Barclays analysts. The brokerage set a $53 price target on Sensata.

data.title: Micron’s ascent to $1T club divides analysts
data.description:
Micron Technology (MU) has drawn a couple of bearish views on Seeking Alpha this week even as the semiconductor firm became the latest entrant to the elite list of U.S. firms with over $1T market capitalization on Tuesday. However, despite its more than 10-fold rally over the past 12 months, Boise, Idaho-based Micron (MU) continues to command an overall bullish view among SA analysts.

The Bulls

“Micron has delivered a nearly 700% 12-month return, underpinned by explosive revenue and margin expansion driven by AI data center demand,” wrote analyst Kenio Fontes in “Micron: Massive Upside, But A Brutal Cycle Eventually Comes.” “Momentum should remain positive near-term, but risk management is crucial given the potential for sharp drawdowns if operational deleverage emerges.”

“I see ~50% upside in MU over 12–24 months, with risks centered on potential demand stalling and rapid memory price corrections,” added Investing Group Leader Danil Sereda in “Micron: Nvidia Gave Us The Green Light To Buy.” “Industry supply constraints until at least 2028 support elevated DRAM/HBM pricing, fueling exceptional margin and free cash flow generation.”

“Admittedly, Micron’s eye-watering rally is increasingly exposing the stock to heightened sensitivity to headline risks—particularly those around memory demand and pricing durability,” notes Summit Research in “Micron: Market Is Still Missing What Nvidia's Fiscal Q1 Just Confirmed.” “Coupled with improving yields and the transition to long-term SCAs, MU remains well-positioned for sustained FCF accretion and further upside from current levels.”

“Micron Technology, Inc. is transitioning from a cyclical memory supplier to a strategic AI infrastructure enabler, driving a re-rating opportunity,” added Agar Capital in “Micron Hits $1 Trillion: This AI Memory Trade Is Not Over.” “I rate MU a Strong Buy with a $1,000 price target, grounded in robust AI-driven demand, durable earnings, and a forward P/E below 8x despite recent price appreciation.”

“Micron Technology guided Q3 revenue toward $33.5 billion with 81% gross margins despite supply remaining constrained beyond 2026,” added Quantryon Capital in “Micron's AI Bottleneck Trade Just Started.” “MU still trades near 15.3x forward earnings despite structurally higher margins, long-term hyperscaler agreements, and AI scarcity economics.”

The Bears

“Micron Technology, Inc. made it into the $1T club, driven by UBS tripling its price target and little to no new fundamental information outside of that,” opined Tech Stock Pros in “Micron: The Market Is Very Wrong.” We expect to see a reality check on the MU Q3 print, specifically from margins, and once expectations reset, see an attractive profile for Micron again.”

“Micron is currently priced for an uninterrupted supply-demand imbalance, leaving zero margin of safety for FY2027's business realities,” added Esxeleryn Analytics in “Micron: Beware Of Trillion-Dollar Trap, HBM4E Cannibalization, And Depreciation Cliff (Rating Downgrade).” “Micron Technology is downgraded to Hold as its $1T+ valuation prices in a perpetual AI supercycle, ignoring looming 2027 margin risks.”

data.title: JPMorgan, Futu in losers; Robinhood, SoFi among gainers: week's financials wrap
data.description:
Wall Street finished the holiday-shortened week on top, with all three major averages advancing as investors weighed signs of easing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, a softer-than-expected inflation reading, and strong corporate earnings that reinforced optimism around AI-driven growth.

However, State Street Financial Sel Sec SPDR ETF (XLF) finished lower, losing 0.69% from the prior week to close at $51.58.

The S&P 500 Index Banks Industry Group (SP500-4010) fell 0.66% during the course of the week, the S&P 500 Diversified Financials Index (SP500-4020) was down 0.04%, and the S&P 500 Insurance Index Industry Group (SP500-4030) slumped 4.05%.

For the individual stocks, Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group (MUFG) led the megacap losers, retreating 3.46% to $18.71.

The week saw media reports on Mitsubishi UFJ Financial exploring options for PT Bank Danamon Indonesia (PBDIF), including a take-private of the lender or selling some of its stake to increase the public float.

Visa (V) and JPMorgan Chase (JPM) followed, with a 1.44% and 1.22% decline to $326.36 and $299.31, respectively.

Visa saw a mixed trading week, declining in two out of the four trading sessions. The stock was falling after the company's presentation at the Bernstein 42nd Annual Strategic Decisions Conference.

The banking giant was in the news time and again during the course of the week.

Wall Street banks, including JPM, were said to be urging the Federal Reserve in efforts behind the scenes to engineer its new supervisory regime so that the changes can't be easily unwound by future administrations.

Specifically, they are asking the U.S. central bank to formally address legal ambiguity regarding the softer process that's replacing "matters requiring attention," or MRAs.

Separately, CEO Jamie Dimon said the bank was on the lookout for acquisitions, and he envisions a $10B-$20B acquisition in the next couple of years.

Furthermore, the week saw Dimon vow that JPMorgan will fight the stablecoin legislation known as the CLARITY Act in its current form. The U.S. Senate Banking Committee had advanced the landmark cryptocurrency legislation to the full Senate for its vote, with the bill now expected to see a fierce lobbying fight.

Goldman Sachs (GS) topped the gainers, advancing 3.78% week-over-week to $1,025.56. COO John Waldron had said merger and acquisition activity is strong so far in 2026, and the company sees the potential to reach a record in advising on M&A.

Additionally, Goldman Sachs' private credit manager said it has sold certain unregistered shares at its net asset value.

Among largecap stocks, Robinhood Markets (HOOD) (+24.21% W/W to $94.30) led the gainers.

The stock climbed after the launch of the Trump Accounts app, which lets Americans open retirement accounts similar to a 401(k) for children.

Hut 8 (HUT) (+18.59% W/W to $124.83) and SoFi Technologies (SOFI) (+16.42% W/W to $18.22) were the other significant gainers in the category.

SOFI jumped after rolling out two major updates that highlighted both stronger consumer spending trends and its deeper push into digital assets.

The week saw the company launch its U.S. dollar stablecoin, SoFiUSD, directly within the SoFi app. The stock also drew investor attention after releasing its first Galileo Debit Spend Index, which showed U.S. consumer spending rebounded sharply in March following a cautious start to Q1.

Everest Group (EG) (-9.10% W/W to $324.03) and Prudential (PUK) (-7.97% W/W to $28.76) were the biggest losers in the category.

For the midcap stocks, Futu Holdings (FUTU) was a notable decliner, sliding 15.98% to $104.07. Futu posted its first-quarter earnings release on Thursday. Prior to the earnings release, the company repurchased its American depositary shares in two tranches as stocks faced price pressure.

StepStone Group (STEP) (-9.92% W/W to $49.31) and SiriusPoint (SPNT) (-9.26% W/W to $21.35) were the other notable laggards among midcap stocks.

Upstart Holdings (UPST) led the gainers, advancing 17.12% to $33.79. The AI lending marketplace said it has updated its Upstart Macro Index to include the April 2026 data.

CleanSpark (CLSK) (+16.05% W/W to $18.29) and Banco Macro (BMA) (+15.89% W/W to $90.78) followed. The bank reported a 131% year-over-year rise in its Q1 net income.

Qfin Holdings (QFIN) (+30.10% W/W to $16.08), Bitgo Holdings (BTGO) (-22.55% W/W to $6.01), and Innventure (INV) (-20.33% W/W to $5.33) were the notable smallcap movers.
